title: thermopsis macrophylla subsp. semota
intro: Thermopsis macrophylla subsp. semota, also known as the golden peavine or mountain tick clover, is a perennial plant species within the Fabaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Thermopsis macrophylla subsp. semota include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve drying and infusing the flowers or leaves to create teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
